item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the simplicity of this one and the fact that it comes from an old phrase (Stop swearing or I'll wash your mouth out with soap!). That said I actually like the idea Photosynthetic mentioned more than the actual articles ending. Still worth a +1 though.

item.title: Suggestion for Name Replacement
item.content:encoded: I think the name of “Censor Bar” or “Censoring Bar” would be a better name than “Hand Soap”, since it is both a pun and briefly hints/describes the SCP’s effects. It also wouldn’t be a weird change of pace to have a pun name, since I’ve seen other SCP names as puns like SCP-3715’s name of “Not So Subtle Tea”.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fits the Factory perfectly. Useful at first, but repeated use is detrimental.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suppose the last bit has the O5s screaming internally because Clef can swear all he wants during meetings

item.title: hahahahahaha
item.content:encoded: This is beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now this is a factory scp, none of that makeup stuff. Factory stuff needs that "Your friends here at the factory" tone in my opinion

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder how does it taste.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The everything-is-censored aspect is actually what sold me. Until then my attitude was more of a mild intrigue.

I like the avenue you've described though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting. I like it. The last addendum's a bit heavy-handed, though. I do like the idea that it expands with repeated use to censor more and more, but I'd be happier without the predictable everything-is-censored endgame. It'd be more interesting if it just censored unpredictably. Or perhaps instead, it could broaden its definition of "expletive" to "anything offensive, in phrasing or idea", or to "anything less-than-diplomatically phrased". Imagine you're a long-term subject, having to invent ever-more-subtle and ever-more-passive-(aggressive) ways of insulting or even disagreeing with people, because the moment anyone is likely to take the slightest umbrage, you're cut off from communication.

No vote for now, but removing that predictable end would earn a swift and enthusiastic upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, mostly because it's good but partly because it's a Factory SCP that doesn't end in gore and tears. Not that I mind gore and tears - it's just a change of pace that still seems like a Foundry item.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That makes perfect sense. I guess I was thinking "kids = Wondertainment", but this isn't a toy, nor is it fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is now my headcanon explanation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought the exact same thing on the first read through, but then I think I realized the difference.

Dr. Wondertainment makes toys. The toys aren't lethal to the child, but could be extremely dangerous to anyone around them.

I'm not sure if The Factory has made anything this cutesy before, but it's a better fit being a mass produced item.

This wouldn't be a fun toy at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because Wondertainment doesn't want to encourage children to swear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*sighs*
So I got to "Know someone with a Dirty mouth?" and read the rest of the addendum in the Orbitz gum commercial-girl's voice.
Other than that, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If not for the recording bit, I'd actually say it should be a -J. But the bleeping recordings for the duration, and "The Factory" make it a good normal SCP.

I will say, it would work as a Wondertainment too. But we need more good factory objects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I saw the first line of the description, went, "Factory soap, ooooh!" and was surprised, but not disappointed. Part of me wonders why this isn't Wondertainment, but it's not like I'm any expert on the differences.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was ok until that last addendum. I can swallow the permanence, but the censor-everything pushes this over the top in a bad way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorts is always glad when it isn't a -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted because I can definitely see this as a Factory product, and because I know some people in real life that I would get a kick out of using it on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Also, makes sense as a Factory item for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sometimes the most literal ones are the best. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1331 was discovered by Foundation agents during a routine visit to an antiques and curiosities shop in [REDACTED], where it was being sold as a 'gag' item.

Imagine how boring it would be for an agent to be assigned the task of regularly sweeping novelty shops for Factory and Dr. Wondertainment items. Maybe that's what the Foundation uses for punishment instead of "Keter duty".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad it's not a -J, it's cute but it has that sinister undertone. I also like the way it bleeps recordings.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not gonna lie, I was listening in on Mann talking about his in chat, and this spontaneously popped into my mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's funny, which makes me think it would be a j, but this works too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are you and Mann working in tandem, with the censorship themed articles? Because if so, that is awesome.
